---
title: "DCA-COVID-beliefs"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-13"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(dplyr)
library(readr)

d_COVID <- read.csv('../data/life-under-lockdown-wave2-extract.csv')
```

# Hypothesis
H1 Gender is associated with number of conspiracy beliefs regarding coronavirus

# Findings
```{r}
d_COVID %>%
  group_by(gender) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    Mdn = median(beliefs),
    M = mean(beliefs),
    SD = sd(beliefs)
  )
```

```{r}
d_COVID %>%
  filter(gender != 'In another way') %>%
  t.test(
    beliefs ~ gender,
    data = .
  )
```

In the sample, males held more conspiracy beliefs regarding coronavirus than female, but this was not statistically significant, t(494.31) = -1.201, p = .230.
